strategicView.summary: Phase 1A is closed. Strategic View now watches whether Phase 1B starts with the language-tutor manifest while keeping secrets, database, and deploy work behind Ask First.

questions: Can S1 start with only the language-tutor manifest, without secrets, database, or deploy mutations?
Will the consumer workflow and install path pin the framework release at v0.1.0?
Does the language-tutor policy profile declare honest coverage before validation?
Can release readiness be blocked unless PostHog flagging, non-stub E2E evidence, cost guardrail proof, and security reviews are complete?
Will Strategic View triage reviewer findings into fix-now work versus post-v0 backlog before any team-facing final report?
Is repository/package cutover to vt-agent-redteam treated as a release gate, not cleanup?
Is the v0.1.0 tag visible and installable for consumers?
Did the package metadata and CLI version agree on 0.1.0?
Is a signed tag required by release policy before SOO consumes this?
Was a real green fixture workflow recorded before closing F6?
Does the reusable workflow preserve manifest validation and stable REDTEAM_RUN_ID?
Which downstream guardrails remain for live secrets, write-results, and schema rollout?
Do PR/deploy/canary modes default to the Langfuse native transcript path?
Does dry-run remain local and safe for fixture validation?
Does non-dry-run fail fast when Langfuse credentials are missing?
Are prompt and response fields redacted before persistence?
Is response_hash computed before redaction for audit stability?
Can expected-behavior allowlists preserve intentionally required spans?
Does P0 remain non-bypassable even with an override?
Are overrides read independently from result writes?
Are transient failures classified below true policy failures?
Does the manifest distinguish full coverage from partial-no-tool-use?
Can scenario_selection exclude unsupported tags without hiding coverage gaps?
Is the coverage state visible before SOO adoption?
Is S1 the right next control point with 1/6 criteria complete?
Are Ask First boundaries still protected before secrets, database, or deploy work?
Does the runner use agent-native transcript evidence instead of synthetic replay?
Is ADR-001 still the decision source for this path?
Are plan constraints still intact before consumer-repo rollout?
Does any new deviation require an ADR before implementation?

Postgres writer invokes redaction before INSERT
Redaction tests cover PII and allowlist

overview.title: Framework boundary
overview.detail: This repo owns the reusable red-team runner, manifest schema, corpus, scoring, storage, and release workflow.

overview.title: SOO boundary
overview.detail: student-onboarding-orchestration consumes the framework for the three LiveKit agents selected for v0.

overview.title: Production moderation
overview.detail: Existing app-level input moderation is not replaced; the MVP validates agent output behavior and deploy safety.

overview.title: Decision posture
overview.detail: Hard constraints, ADRs, Ask First rules, and bilingual-doc conventions remain binding for every new step.

risks.title: Langfuse correlation metadata
risks.detail: Plan assumes redteam_run_id and redteam_scenario_id metadata. SOO tracing may need explicit metadata wiring before runner search works reliably.

risks.title: SOO Ask First boundaries
risks.detail: DB, auth, deploy, infrastructure, and destructive operations require explicit confirmation inside the consumer repo.

risks.title: Maya tool-use coverage gap
risks.detail: support-agent must declare partial-no-tool-use until scenario/tool traces are proven.

risks.title: Supabase deploy-killer rules
risks.detail: Conversation Club schema work must follow local migration rules and avoid direct remote mutations.

risks.title: Deferred audio and BI scope
risks.detail: WAV collector D1 and BI dashboard D9 remain post-v0 unless priorities change through a new ADR.

commits.message: fix(dashboard): focus strategic view on questions
